questions.text: Which of the following phenomenon proves that light waves are transverse waves?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Polarization refers to the orientation of oscillations in a transverse wave. When light waves are polarized, it means that the oscillations of the electric field (and magnetic field, in the case of electromagnetic waves) occur in a specific direction. For example, if light is polarized vertically, the electric field oscillates vertically.Polarization can occur when light interacts with certain materials, such as polarizing filters. These filters allow light waves oscillating in a particular direction to pass through while blocking light oscillating in other directions.The phenomenon of polarization demonstrates that light behaves as a transverse wave because its oscillations occur perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another with a different optical density. This bending occurs due to a change in the speed of light when transitioning between media.

Interference occurs when two or more light waves combine to form a resultant wave. This can result in constructive interference (where waves reinforce each other) or destructive interference (where waves cancel each other out).

Diffraction refers to the bending of waves around obstacles and the spreading of waves as they pass through narrow openings.When light encounters an obstacle or passes through a narrow slit, it bends around the edges, resulting in a diffraction pattern. This pattern is characteristic of wave behavior.

questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can undergo polarisation. Longitudinal waves cannot undergo polarization. Since light can undergo polarisation, it is a transverse wave.In summary, while refraction, interference, and diffraction are all phenomena that demonstrate the wave nature of light, they do not specifically prove that light waves are transverse waves. Each of these phenomena can be understood within the broader framework of wave optics, which encompasses both transverse and longitudinal waves.

questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Motion of electric and magnetic fields
options.explanation: An oscillating charge produces an oscillating electric field in space which further produces an oscillating magnetic field which in turn is a source of electric field.
Production of EM waves requires charged particles.
EM waves are produced by moving particles.
Thermal radiation is generated when heat from the movement of charges in the material (electrons and protons in common forms of matter) is converted to electromagnetic radiation.
This option is incorrect

questions.explanation: Electromagnetic waves are the combination of electric and magnetic field waves produced by moving charges.The creation of all electromagnetic waves begins with a charged particle. This charged particle creates an electric field (which can exert a force on other nearby charged particles). When it accelerates as part of an oscillatory motion, the charged particle creates ripples, or oscillations, in its electric field, and also produces a magnetic field (as predicted by Maxwell’s equation).

questions.text: A star moving away from earth shows?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Green shift is not a term used in astronomy. The common terms used are "red shift" and "blue shift," which refer to the apparent change in the wavelength of light from a celestial object due to its motion relative to the observer.

Red shift occurs when a celestial object is moving away from the observer (in this case, Earth). As the object moves away, the wavelength of its light appears to stretch out or increase, shifting towards the longer-wavelength (redder) end of the spectrum. Red shift is a key observation in cosmology and is associated with the expansion of the universe.

Blue shift occurs when a celestial object is moving towards the observer. As the object moves closer, the wavelength of its light appears to compress or decrease, shifting towards the shorter-wavelength (bluer) end of the spectrum. Blue shift is also observed in cosmology, especially in cases of objects moving towards us, such as certain stars or galaxies.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: This option is not correct because either red shift or blue shift will be observed depending on the direction of the object's motion relative to the observer (Earth). The correct answer to the question is one of the two shifts (red shift or blue shift) depending on the direction of motion of the star.

questions.explanation: When a star emits light, the color of its light as observed on earth depends on its motion relative to earth. If a star is moving towards the earth then its light is shifted to higher frequencies on the color spectrum (towards the gamma-ray end of the spectrum). This shift towards higher frequency is called a "blue shift". The faster a star moves towards the earth, the more its light is shifted to higher frequencies. In contrast, if a star is moving away from the earth, its light is shifted to lower frequencies on the color spectrum (towards the orange/red/infrared/microwave/radio end of the spectrum). A lower frequency shift is called a "redshift". The faster a star moves away from the earth, the more its light is shifted to lower-frequency colors. This effect is known as the "Doppler shift".

questions.text: Which one of the following requires no medium?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Beta rays consist of high-energy electrons or positrons emitted from the nucleus of certain radioactive atoms during beta decay.Beta rays are charged particles and require a medium to propagate. They can travel through a vacuum but interact with matter, causing ionization and energy loss.

Alpha rays are composed of alpha particles, which are helium-4 nuclei consisting of two protons and two neutrons.Similar to beta rays, alpha particles are charged and require a medium to propagate. They have a relatively low penetration ability and can be stopped by a few centimeters of air or a sheet of paper.

Gamma rays are electromagnetic waves with the highest frequency and energy in the electromagnetic spectrum.Unlike alpha and beta particles, gamma rays are not composed of particles and do not carry an electric charge. They are pure energy.Gamma rays can travel through a vacuum, as well as through air and other materials, with minimal interaction. They require no medium to propagate and can penetrate deeply into materials.
This option is incorrect as per the explanation

questions.explanation: Out of all these, Gamma rays are an example of electromagnetic waves, which require NO medium to travel and propagate from one place to the other or even through a vacuum.On the other hand, mechanical waves require a medium to travel and propagate from one place to the other and can NOT pass through the vacuum. Examples of mechanical waves are sound waves, alpha rays, beta rays, etc.The picture attached represents the 'Electromagnetic Spectrum', of which 'Gamma Rays' are a part of.

questions.text: In water pipes, _ is used to detect leakages.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Light is not typically used to detect leakages in water pipes. While optical methods might be employed in certain contexts for inspecting pipes, they are not the primary means for detecting leaks, especially within the pipes themselves where light might not penetrate effectively.

Radiation, particularly methods involving the use of radioactive tracers or isotopes, can be employed to detect leakages in water pipes. This method involves introducing a small amount of a radioactive substance into the water and then using radiation detectors to locate any leaks based on the presence of the radioactive material.

Electrical current is not commonly used to detect leakages in water pipes. While electrical methods might be employed for certain purposes in pipeline inspection, they are not typically used specifically for leak detection.

While other methods might be used in certain contexts for detecting leaks in water pipes, the use of radiation is indeed a common and effective technique for this purpose. Other methods might include acoustic methods, pressure testing, or visual inspection, but radiation-based methods are frequently used for accurate and non-invasive detection of leaks.

questions.explanation: Substances that emit gamma radiation are used as tracers. To find a leak or blockage, the radioactive material is put into one end of the pipe. A radiation detector outside the pipe or above ground is used to track its progress through the pipe. The leak or blockage is discovered by finding where the amount of radiation detected decreases.

questions.text: The region that lies between IR and UV regions is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Radio waves have wavelengths longer than infrared light and are typically associated with communication technologies such as radio broadcasting, telecommunications, and radar. They lie at the lower end of the electromagnetic spectrum, well below the infrared region.

This option implies that the region between IR and UV has no specific designation, which is not correct. This region indeed has a specific name, as it encompasses a range of wavelengths with distinct properties.

X-rays have much shorter wavelengths and higher frequencies than ultraviolet (UV) light. They are typically used in medical imaging and security scanning due to their ability to penetrate various materials. X-rays lie well beyond the ultraviolet region on the electromagnetic spectrum.

Visible light is the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum that is visible to the human eye. It ranges in wavelength from approximately 400 nanometers (nm) to 700 nm, spanning colors from violet to red. This region lies between the infrared and ultraviolet regions.

This option suggests that all the mentioned regions (radio waves, X-ray region, and visible light) lie between IR and UV, which is incorrect. Radio waves are below IR, and X-rays are beyond UV. Visible light is the only region that fits between IR and UV.

questions.explanation: The visible light region falls between the ultraviolet (UV) and infrared (IR) regions on the electromagnetic spectrum. The UV region has a wavelength in the range of 100-400 nm whereas the IR region has a wavelength in the range of 760-1 million nm. The narrow range of 400-700 nm represents rays in the visible light range; rays of wavelengths visible to the human eye.

questions.text: Which one of the following waves needs a medium to travel?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: X-rays are a form of electromagnetic radiation with very short wavelengths and high frequencies. They do not require a medium to travel and can propagate through a vacuum.

Gamma rays are also a form of electromagnetic radiation, but they have even shorter wavelengths and higher frequencies than X-rays. Like X-rays, gamma rays do not require a medium to travel and can propagate through a vacuum.
This option implies that none of the waves mentioned require a medium to travel. While it's true for X-rays and gamma rays, it's not accurate for beta rays.

Beta rays consist of high-energy electrons or positrons emitted from the nucleus of certain radioactive atoms during beta decay. Beta particles are charged and have mass, so they require a medium to travel. They can travel through a vacuum but interact with matter, causing ionization and energy loss.

questions.explanation: Beta particles, also known as beta rays, are high speed, high energy electron (β-) or positron (β+) emitted from the radioactive decay of the atomic nucleus. This process is called beta decay. These rays need a medium to travel. On the other hand, electromagnetic waves are waves that do not require a medium to travel. Examples of EM waves include gamma, X, infra-red ways, etc.

questions.text: Electron charge in accelerating motion will produce _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An accelerating electron does produce an electric field, but this field is a static field associated with the charge of the electron itself. It does not directly result from the accelerating motion of the electron.

Similarly, an accelerating electron does produce a magnetic field, but this field is also static and associated with the charge of the electron itself. It is not a direct consequence of the accelerating motion of the electron.

This option is correct because accelerating charges produce changing electric fields and magnetic fields. Changing electric fields produces magnetic fields and changing magnetic fields produces electric fields. This interplay between the induced electric field and magnetic field leads to the propagation of electromagnetic waves.
This option is incorrect because, as explained, the accelerating motion of an electron does produce electromagnetic waves (option C).

questions.explanation: Correct Option: CThis option is correct because accelerating charges produce changing electric fields and magnetic fields. Changing electric fields produces magnetic fields and changing magnetic fields produces electric fields. This interplay between the induced electric field and magnetic field leads to propagation of electromagnetic waves

questions.text: In microwave ovens _ is used to heat the food
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Microwave ovens heat food using microwaves, a form of electromagnetic radiation similar to radio waves.
This option is incorrect. Microwave ovens heat food using microwaves, a form of electromagnetic radiation similar to radio waves.
This option is incorrect. Microwave ovens heat food using microwaves, a form of electromagnetic radiation similar to radio waves.

Microwave ovens heat food using microwaves, a form of electromagnetic radiation similar to radio waves. Microwaves have three characteristics that allow them to be used in cooking: they are reflected by metal; they pass through glass, paper, plastic, and similar materials; and they are absorbed by foods.

questions.explanation: Microwave ovens heat food using microwaves, a form of electromagnetic radiation similar to radio waves. Microwaves have three characteristics that allow them to be used in cooking: they are reflected by metal; they pass through glass, paper, plastic, and similar materials; and they are absorbed by foods.OPTIONS A, B & C: They might cause the food to become radioactive so these options are incorrect.

questions.text: Ozone reflects _ radiation from sun back into space.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Ozone is not particularly effective at reflecting infrared radiation. Infrared radiation is absorbed and emitted by molecules in the atmosphere, including ozone, but it is not primarily reflected by ozone back into space.
Higher up, in the stratosphere, the ozone layer absorbs solar ultraviolet radiation and affects how much of the Sun's heat is radiated back into space.

This option is incorrect. Alpha radiation consists of alpha particles, which are helium-4 nuclei composed of two protons and two neutrons. Alpha radiation is not relevant in the context of ozone and solar radiation interactions in the Earth's atmosphere.

This option is incorrect. This option suggests that ozone reflects all types of radiation (IR, UV, Alpha) from the sun back into space, which is not accurate. While ozone does interact with various types of radiation, its primary role in the atmosphere is absorbing and scattering UV radiation, particularly UV-B and UV-C.

questions.explanation: Ozone is known for its ability to absorb and scatter ultraviolet radiation from the sun. In the Earth's atmosphere, the ozone layer absorbs a significant portion of the incoming UV radiation, particularly UV-B and UV-C, protecting life on Earth from harmful effects of excessive UV exposure. Therefore, ozone primarily reflects UV radiation back into space.OPTIONS A & C: Ozone doesn’t reflect or absorb IV and Alpha radiation hence these options are incorrect.

questions.text: Energy emitted in the form of visible radiation is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The energy emitted in the form of visible radiation is called light energy. Light energy refers to the energy which enables us to have a vision. It is the energy that helps us to see. We can see the radiation of light energy from the natural source of the sun and the artificial sources of bulbs and tube lights. Hence, it is the answer.
Sound energy enables us to hear when the sound waves reflect off our eardrums.
Tidal energy is the energy that is generated from the tides and the surges of water.
Geothermal energy is the energy that is extracted from within the earth. The heat of the earth is extracted and used to keep buildings warm, generate electricity, and so on.

questions.explanation: Light energy comes in the form of visible radiation.﻿The energy emitted in the form of visible radiation is called light energy. Light energy refers to the energy which enables us to have a vision. It is the energy that helps us to see. We can see the radiation of light energy from the natural source of the sun and the artificial sources of bulbs and tube lights. Hence, it is the answer.

questions.text: The electromagnetic radiation which moves with high frequency will have _ wavelength:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Short wavelength: Electromagnetic radiation with high frequency will have a short wavelength. Frequency and wavelength are inversely proportional, meaning that as the frequency increases, the wavelength decreases. This is a fundamental relationship in wave physics.
b) Long wavelength: This is not correct. As mentioned above, electromagnetic radiation with higher frequency has shorter wavelengths, not longer ones.
c) Unchanged: This is not correct. The wavelength of electromagnetic radiation is inversely proportional to its frequency, so if the frequency is high, the wavelength will be short.
d) Variable: This is not correct. The relationship between frequency and wavelength is fixed and predictable. Higher frequency always corresponds to shorter wavelengths, and vice versa.

questions.explanation: In electromagnetic wave spectrum frequency and wavelength are inversely proportional.
